question.text: Which of the following indices uses a price-weighted methodology?

answer.text: Dow Jones Industrial Average and Nikkei 225

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) and Nikkei 225 are the most well-known examples of price-weighted indices. In a price-weighted index, stocks with higher share prices have more influence on the index, regardless of the company's total market value. **The Trap:** Sensex and Nifty 50 use free-float market capitalization weighting. S&P 500 and FTSE 100 also use market cap weighting. Nifty Bank and Nifty IT are sectoral indices using free-float market cap weighting. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII requires knowledge of different index construction methodologies. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Price-weighted = DJIA, Nikkei 225 (higher priced stocks dominate). Market-cap weighted = Sensex, Nifty, S&P 500 (larger companies dominate).
